ogTitle: Jeffcott North vs Jericho: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Jeffcott North and Jericho.
heading: Jeffcott North vs Jericho — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Jeffcott North, Victoria and Jericho, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Jericho has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Jeffcott North (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Jeffcott North or Jericho safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Jeffcott North has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Jericho scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Jeffcott North scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Jericho scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Jeffcott North or Jericho?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Jericho has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
